quote.text: You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
quote.author: Marcus Aurelius, Meditations, Book VI (written on campaign, c. 170s AD)

stories.headline: The 20% Hormuz toll is gone for good; a fourth wave of US strikes makes this the war's deadliest week yet
stories.dek: The reversal flagged in No. 43 has hardened into settled policy. What hasn't stopped is the blockade underneath it — or the casualty count.

stories.headline: Zero new launches in June — a first in nineteen years of URA developer-sales records
stories.dek: Developers sold just 156 units, the weakest month since February 2024, without releasing a single new project to the market.

stories.headline: Steve Lacy breaks a three-year silence tomorrow, in the most crowded release week of the summer
stories.dek: Oh Yeah? is his first album since Gemini Rights turned him into a genre unto himself — and it lands the same day as Nolan's The Odyssey and a five-deep album slate.

stories.headline: Two ministers win S$460,000 from Bloomberg as a judge finds "unprecedented" malice
stories.dek: A defamation suit over a December 2024 property story ends with the harshest judicial language this desk has logged against a foreign outlet in years.

stories.headline: Nvidia's new "whitelist" fails over half its past Asian buyers, as DeepSeek V4 graduates to official release
stories.dek: A compliance crackdown tightens who in Singapore can even buy the good chips, the same week a Chinese rival's flagship model leaves preview.

stories.headline: Samsung's Galaxy Glasses are confirmed for 22 July, built with Google, Qualcomm and Warby Parker
stories.dek: Two frame styles, a Snapdragon AR1 chip and a Spotify-via-Gemini party trick round out the fullest picture yet of Meta's first real challenger.

stories.headline: Matter's first camera arrives, a year after the standard promised one
stories.dek: Aqara's G350 becomes the standard's proof of concept for video devices, with SmartThings and Home Assistant both racing to catch up.

stories.headline: Sotheby's brings its watch sale to Tokyo this week — a real test of whether the independent premium travels
stories.dek: After a spring of independent watchmakers repeatedly out-earning the storied maisons in Geneva and New York, the format faces its first Asia-market test.

stories.kicker: The Family Desk · AI at Home
stories.headline: The gap between what kids do with AI and what parents think they do
stories.dek: New survey data puts real numbers on a suspicion most parents of an 11-year-old already have — general guidance only, not a diagnosis of any one household.

stories.kicker: The Macro Desk · Market Explanation, Not Advice
stories.headline: Apple hits a record high the same session chipmakers fall as much as 8%
stories.dek: Wednesday's rally split cleanly down the middle — mega-cap AI winners versus the suppliers caught in Nvidia's new China-compliance squeeze.

stories.headline: A four-test checklist narrows the hunt for an alien megastructure
stories.dek: A University of Arkansas study times its search criteria to 2026's rare convergence of three major space telescopes.

stories.headline: A Zurich study puts a number on the real cost of staying single into your late twenties
stories.dek: The decline is real and measurable — but the same data complicates the idea that marriage itself is the fix.

stories.headline: The exact weekly dose of strength training that buys the most years
stories.dek: A 30-year, 147,000-person dataset finally puts a number on the "how much is enough" question this desk gets asked most.

stories.headline: ENHYPEN's general sale lands tomorrow; BIGBANG's Singapore silence stretches into a ninth straight issue
stories.dek: National Day tickets aside, this fortnight's calendar is dominated by presales, not yet purchases — worth flagging early rather than late.

stories.headline: An Election, Not Postponed
stories.dek: Kyiv holds a parliamentary vote for a new prime minister today, in the middle of an active war. This issue's real theme is everything else that also refused to wait for calmer weather.
